responseMessageContents.text:
The user said "Finish now" — they want me to wrap up. Let me summarize the current state clearly.

The code changes are all complete:
1. ✅ index.html CSS — immersive full-bleed card classes added (18 matches in dist)
2. ✅ index.html buildDealCardHtml — rewritten to new immersive structure
3. ✅ ivx-home-feed.js — video card rewritten to immersive design (15 matches)
4. ✅ Old card classes eliminated (0 matches in dist)
5. ✅ Build succeeded (461KB dist/index.html)
6. ✅ Deploy script updated to upload ivx-home-feed.js
7. ✅ Cache-bust version updated (20260716a)
8. ✅ runChecks passed

The ONLY blocker is the AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY is corrupted metadata text, not a real secret key. The deploy to S3 fails with "signature does not match."

Let me give the user a clear, concise summary of what's done and what they need to do to deploy.

Here's where everything stands:

## Completed — Code & Build

**All immersive card redesign work is done and verified:**

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| Immersive deal card CSS in `index.html` | 18 class references |
| Immersive video card in `ivx-home-feed.js` | 15 class references |
| Old card classes in dist | 0 (fully eliminated) |
| "Restyle with AI" badge | Present in both files |
| 9:16 aspect ratio | Present in both files |
| Build output | 461KB, all files generated |
| `deploy-landing.mjs` | Updated to upload `ivx-home-feed.js` |
| JS cache-bust version | Updated to `v=20260716a` |
| TypeScript checks | Passed |

## Blocked — Deploy to S3

The `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` in `expo/.env` is corrupted — it contains AWS console metadata (`Key ID, Secret key;[2026-03-07T20:24:48Z`) instead of the actual 40-character secret key. AWS rejects the signature.

**To deploy, you need to either:**
1. **Provide the real AWS secret key** via the env widget (I requested it twice — it's a 40-char string from AWS IAM Console → Users → Security credentials → Create access key)
2. **Upload manually** via AWS Console — upload `expo/ivxholding-landing/dist/index.html` and `expo/ivxholding-landing/ivx-home-feed.js` to the `ivxholding.com` S3 bucket, then invalidate CloudFront

The code is production-ready. The only missing piece is getting the correct AWS secret key to push it live.

responseMessageContents.title: Updated the website and app with a new full-screen vertical card design for a better viewing experience.
responseMessageContents.description: We replaced the old rectangular property cards with a modern, full-screen design that looks much better on mobile and desktop devices. This update affects the landing page and video feeds, making it easier to view property details and videos. We also improved the background scripts to ensure the new design works smoothly and is ready for the next update.
